hypothesis: A harness that records memory operations can separate answer correctness from retrieval quality, update handling, stale-fact suppression, selective forgetting, provenance, and multi-session handoff behavior.

metricDefinitions.name: Memory quality score
metricDefinitions.description: Average memory-operation quality across retrieval, stale suppression, forgetting, provenance, and handoff dimensions.

metricDefinitions.name: Answer correct rate
metricDefinitions.description: Share of memory tasks with a useful final answer.

metricDefinitions.name: Retrieval miss count
metricDefinitions.description: Relevant memory references that were expected but not retrieved.

metricDefinitions.name: Stale fact use count
metricDefinitions.description: Superseded memory references used in an answer or memory decision.

metricDefinitions.name: Leaked forgotten ref count
metricDefinitions.description: Forgotten or tombstoned memory references that leaked into a response.

metricDefinitions.name: Provenance coverage
metricDefinitions.description: Share of memory cases with provenance evidence.

cases.rationale: The answer should retrieve both current user preference and project deadline records.
The current preference must supersede the older channel preference.
A stale vendor status should be detected and excluded from the answer.
A tombstoned personal detail must not be surfaced after a forgetting request.
The final explanation should cite the message and run artifacts that support the memory.
A later session should preserve and use a prior-session finding without re-deriving it from scratch.
